_schema_docs.description: Technology tree nodes for bootstrapping industrial civilization from stone-age materials to semiconductor manufacturing. Replaces the former dependencies.json with a unified, hierarchy-aware structure that merges core phases and side quests into a single ontology.

hierarchy.domain: Top-level technology area (e.g., 'metallurgy', 'textiles', 'silicon'). Has no parent.
hierarchy.capability: Major skill or process family within a domain (e.g., 'metallurgy.smelting', 'textiles.spinning'). Parent is a domain.
hierarchy.process: Specific technique or operation (e.g., 'metallurgy.smelting.blast-furnace', 'textiles.spinning.drop-spindle'). Parent is a capability.

_schema_docs.id_format: Dotted hierarchical using kebab-case segments: 'domain', 'domain.capability', 'domain.capability.process'. The number of dots indicates depth: 0 dots = domain, 1 dot = capability, 2+ dots = process.

fields.id: Unique dotted hierarchical identifier. Must match the node's position in the tree.
fields.name: Human-readable display name.
fields.parent: ID of the parent node, or null for domain-level nodes.
fields.description: 1-3 sentence summary of what this node represents and why it matters.
fields.timeline: Estimated time range as a string (e.g., 'Years 0-10', 'Years 70-200+'). Inherited/approximate for capability and process levels.
fields.outputs: Array of product/material/capability tokens produced by this node. Referenced by edges in edges.json to define dependencies between nodes.
fields.critical: Boolean. True if this node is a critical-path enabler without which downstream technology is blocked.
fields.critical_note: String. Why this node is critical. Only present when critical=true.
fields.early_win: Boolean. True if this node provides a high-value output early, creating a positive feedback loop.
fields.early_win_note: String. Description of the feedback loop. Only present when early_win=true.
fields.pinnacle: Boolean. True if this node is an end-goal requiring the entire mature ecosystem plus generations of refinement.
fields.pinnacle_note: String. Why this is a pinnacle achievement. Only present when pinnacle=true.
fields.taxonomy: Optional array of dotted hierarchical strings for material-origin classification. Each value must have ≥2 kebab-case segments (e.g., 'biomass.plant.wood'). First segment must be one of: 'biomass', 'mineral', 'synthetic'. See schema-spec.md §4 for full specification.

notes: Outputs are orphan tokens — they become meaningful when edges.json (Task 3) connects them via 'requires' and 'produces' relationships.
The schema unifies former 'phases' and 'side quests' into a single hierarchy. No phase_type, phase_number, sq_number, or main_quest/side_quest fields exist.
Binary dependencies only (required/not) — no soft/optional dependency grading.
When populating (Task 6), ensure every capability.process parent chain resolves to an existing node.
The taxonomy field is optional and independent from tags. It provides hierarchical material-origin classification using dotted strings (biomass.*, mineral.*, synthetic.*). See schema-spec.md §4.

nodes.name: Foundations
nodes.description: Fire-making, stone and wood tools, and agriculture. The starting point for all civilization bootstrapping — no surplus means no specialist labor means no industry.
nodes.critical_note: Everything depends on food surplus to free specialist labor and fire for all thermal processes
nodes.early_win_note: Fire + food surplus → specialist labor → better tools → more surplus

nodes.name: Fire-Making
nodes.description: Fire-making techniques: friction fire, flint-and-steel ignition, fire maintenance, and heat management.

nodes.name: Stone & Wood Tools
nodes.description: Flint knapping (hard hammer, soft hammer, pressure flaking), ground stone tools, and wooden implements with hafting using birch tar adhesive. Produces scrapers, axes, drills, and digging sticks for all early construction and material extraction.

nodes.name: Agriculture & Food Production
nodes.description: Grain cultivation, animal domestication, food preservation (drying, smoking, salting, fermentation), and irrigation. The food surplus loop — more food → more specialists → better tools → more food — is the engine that drives all subsequent development.

nodes.name: Metals
nodes.description: Extraction and working of metals from ore. Spans copper, bronze, iron, and steel production with increasingly complex smelting.

nodes.name: Copper & Bronze Production
nodes.description: Copper smelting from malachite, azurite, and chalcopyrite in forced-draft charcoal furnaces at 1085-1200°C. Bronze alloying with 5-12% tin for harder, more castable tools. Sand, clay, and stone mold casting techniques.

nodes.name: Iron & Steel Production
nodes.description: Bloomery smelting of iron ore at 1200-1400°C producing wrought iron, carburization for steel, and crucible steel (Wootz method). Heat treatment: annealing, quenching, and tempering. This is the critical metallurgical capability that enables all subsequent domains.
nodes.critical_note: Iron and steel are essential for machine tool beds, cutting tools, boilers, generators, and all precision machinery

nodes.name: Basic Glass Production
nodes.description: Basic glass production from silica sand, potash, and limestone at 1100-1400°C for vessels, beads, and window panes.

nodes.name: Machine Tools Bootstrap
nodes.description: Precision manufacturing capability built via Gingery-style iterative bootstrap: casting → crude lathe → improved lathe → shaper → mill → drill press → surface grinder. Covers metal forming, machining, joining, and casting. The single most important parallel track — without machine tools, no semiconductor equipment can be built.
nodes.critical_note: Master enabler: every later stage depends on precision parts — cylinders, leadscrews, slides, seals

nodes.name: Metal Casting
nodes.description: Crucible furnaces for aluminum (660°C) and cast iron (1200-1400°C), green sand molding with bentonite binder, pattern making with shrinkage allowance, flask-based casting, and investment casting. Every machine tool starts as a sand-cast iron or aluminum casting.

nodes.name: Iterative Machine Bootstrap
nodes.description: The core self-improving sequence: crude lathe (0.5 mm tolerance) → improved lathe with leadscrew (0.1 mm) → shaper (0.05 mm) → milling machine (0.025 mm) → drill press → surface grinder (0.005 mm). Each machine doubles precision achievable with the previous generation.

nodes.name: Precision Metrology & Standards
nodes.description: Surface plates via Whitworth three-plate method, hand scraping, straight edges, squares, vernier calipers (0.02 mm), micrometers (0.01 mm), gauge blocks (Jo-blocks), go/no-go gauges, and thread standards. Includes base unit standards, timekeeping, calibration infrastructure, electrical standards, and optical metrology. You cannot build what you cannot measure.

nodes.name: Bearings, Abrasives & Cutting Tools
nodes.description: Plain journal bearings (babbitt metal), rolling element ball bearings (52100 steel), natural abrasives (emery, sandstone) and synthetic abrasives (SiC, Al₂O₃ from electric arc furnace), cutting tool materials (carbon steel, HSS), thread cutting on lathe, and tap and die production.

nodes.name: Metal Forming
nodes.description: Forge work, hammer forming, rolling, and drawing of metals into shapes.

nodes.name: Machining
nodes.description: Lathe turning, milling, drilling, and grinding of metal parts to precise dimensions.

nodes.name: Metal Joining
nodes.description: Forge welding, brazing, soldering, and riveting of metal components. Also covers oxy-acetylene welding and cutting (3100°C flame) and arc welding (SMAW/stick) with flux-coated electrodes.

nodes.name: Energy
nodes.description: Water and wind power, solid fuel production, steam engines, electromagnetic generation, electric furnaces, cooling and refrigeration, and energy storage. The energy revolution transforms manufacturing from muscle-powered to machine-powered industry.

nodes.name: Water Power
nodes.description: Undershot and overshot water wheels (1-5 HP) using gravitational water flow for mechanical power.

nodes.name: Wind Power
nodes.description: Windmills for mechanical power: grain grinding, sawing, and water pumping.

nodes.name: Fuel Production
nodes.description: Solid, liquid, and gaseous fuel production from coal, coke, charcoal, and petroleum sources.

nodes.name: Charcoal Production
nodes.description: Charcoal production via earth-covered pit and simple kiln methods. Primary fuel for all metallurgy until coke arrives.

nodes.name: Coal Mining & Combustion
nodes.description: Bituminous and anthracite coal mining, preparation, and combustion for steam generation and industrial heating.

nodes.name: Coke Production
nodes.description: Beehive and by-product recovery coke ovens converting bituminous coal to coke (60-70% yield). Coke burns at 1800-2100°C.

nodes.name: Steam Power
nodes.description: Newcomen atmospheric engine (5-15 HP, 0.5-1% efficiency), Watt separate condenser engine (10-100+ HP, 3-5% efficiency), boiler construction from riveted iron plate, safety valves, and power transmission from reciprocating to rotary motion.

nodes.name: Heat Engines
nodes.description: Internal and external combustion engines for mechanical power generation. Otto cycle (gasoline) and diesel cycle engines convert fuel combustion into rotary mechanical power with higher power-to-weight ratios than steam engines. Enables motorized transport, aviation, and portable power generation.
nodes.critical_note: Internal combustion engines enable motorized transport, aviation, and portable power generation that steam engines cannot practically provide

nodes.name: Electricity Generation & Distribution
nodes.description: Voltaic piles and Daniell cells, wire drawing, generators (Faraday magneto → self-exciting dynamo), DC and AC motors, transformers for voltage step-up/step-down, and power distribution (AC wins long-term). Copper wire gauge reference and motor/generator sizing included.

nodes.name: Electric Furnaces
nodes.description: Electric arc furnace (EAF) for steelmaking and silicon reduction, submerged arc furnace for continuous production, and resistance heating elements (Nichrome, Kanthal, SiC). Arc temperatures reach 3000-3500°C. EAF is critical for MG-Si production and specialty steel.

nodes.name: Cooling Systems & Refrigeration
nodes.description: Refrigeration cycles, ice production, cooling systems for industrial processes and climate control.

nodes.name: Energy Storage & Diversification
nodes.description: Lead-acid batteries, nickel-iron batteries, mechanical storage (pumped hydro, flywheels, compressed air), energy diversification (hydroelectric, wind, solar PV, biomass), grid infrastructure, and UPS systems for semiconductor fab equipment. Semiconductor fabrication cannot tolerate power interruptions.

nodes.name: Chemistry
nodes.description: Bulk mineral acids, alkalis, industrial gases, distillation infrastructure, cement production, air separation, specialty gases for semiconductor fabrication, petroleum alternatives and coal tar chemistry, lubricants and cutting fluids, and electrolytic processes. A civilization's industrial maturity can be measured by its sulfuric acid production. Provides the etchants, solvents, reagents, and purification infrastructure needed for semiconductor fabrication.

nodes.name: Mineral Acid Production
nodes.description: Sulfuric acid (lead chamber process 65-70%, contact process 96-98%), nitric acid (from nitrates + H₂SO₄, later Ostwald process), hydrochloric acid (NaCl + H₂SO₄ or direct H₂ + Cl₂ synthesis), and hydrofluoric acid (CaF₂ + H₂SO₄, extremely hazardous). HF is uniquely dangerous and essential for glass and silicon etching.

nodes.name: Alkali Production
nodes.description: Leblanc process (first synthetic soda ash) and Solvay process (more efficient, later) for Na₂CO₃, lime-soda process and chlor-alkali electrolysis for NaOH. Soda ash and caustic soda are essential for glass making, soap production, paper pulping, and chemical processing.

nodes.name: Cement & Concrete
nodes.description: Portland cement from limestone + clay heated to 1450°C, ground with gypsum. Concrete (1:2:4 cement:sand:gravel mix), reinforced concrete with steel rebar, and curing protocols. Dominant building material for factory foundations, dams, roads, and vibration isolation pads.

nodes.name: Electrolysis
nodes.description: Electrolytic cells for chlor-alkali (NaCl → Cl₂ + NaOH), water splitting (H₂ + O₂), aluminum Hall-Héroult process, and copper refining. DC power at 2-5V per cell, industrial current 100-10,000+ A. Enables production of ultra-pure hydrogen, chlorine, and aluminum.

nodes.name: Air Separation & Bulk Gas Production
nodes.description: Linde fractional distillation of liquid air producing nitrogen (99-99.99%), oxygen (95-99.5%), and argon (99-99.999%). Argon is critical for CZ crystal growth inert atmosphere. Double-column distillation at cryogenic temperatures with Joule-Thomson cooling.

nodes.name: Hydrogen & Silane Production
nodes.description: Hydrogen via electrolysis (4-5 kWh/Nm³) with Pd membrane purification, steam reforming of methane, and silane (SiH₄) production from MG-Si via trichlorosilane redistribution. Silane is pyrophoric — rigorous handling protocols required.

nodes.name: Dopant & Etch Gases
nodes.description: Dopant gases (PH₃, AsH₃, B₂H₆ — extremely toxic, lethal at ppm concentrations) and etch gases (CF₄, SF₆, Cl₂, F₂). Fluorine production by KF·2HF electrolysis. Exhaust gas abatement via thermal oxidation and wet scrubbing mandatory for all dopant gas use.

nodes.name: Semiconductor Packaging & Testing
nodes.description: Wafer backgrinding, die singulation (diamond blade dicing), die attach (eutectic or epoxy), wire bonding (Au ball bonding, Al wedge), encapsulation (transfer molding with epoxy-silica compound), lead forming, wafer probing (DC parametric + functional), burn-in, and final test.

nodes.name: Petroleum & Alternative Chemistry
nodes.description: Petroleum extraction (surface seeps → cable-tool → rotary drilling), refining (batch distillation → fractional distillation → thermal cracking), coal tar chemistry (benzene, phenol, naphthalene from coke ovens), fermentation (ethanol, acetone, acetic acid, methanol), and wood gasification. Dual-path strategy: petroleum accelerates everything, but coal tar + fermentation covers the same molecules.

nodes.name: Lubricants, Oils & Fluid Mechanics
nodes.description: Animal fats and vegetable oils, lubrication regimes (boundary, hydrodynamic, EHL), cutting fluids for machining, grease production (calcium, sodium, lithium soap), bearing lubrication methods, hydraulic fluids, vacuum oils (mineral, silicone), and cleanroom-compatible lubricants. Without lubrication, every moving part seizes.
nodes.critical_note: Without lubrication, bearings gall, slides bind, gears wear out in hours, and cutting tools destroy both workpiece and themselves

nodes.name: Advanced Glass Production
nodes.description: Borosilicate glass (Pyrex-type, thermal shock resistant), fused silica/quartz glass (1700°C+, critical for CZ crucibles), glass tubing production, glassworking techniques (lampworking, sealing), and quartz crucible fabrication for semiconductor use.

nodes.name: Vacuum Technology
nodes.description: Piston pumps (rough vacuum 10-50 Torr), rotary vane pumps (workhorse, 10⁻²-10⁻³ Torr), diffusion pumps (high vacuum 10⁻⁶-10⁻⁸ Torr), vacuum chambers (steel or glass), vacuum measurement (Bourdon, Pirani, ion gauges), and leak detection (bubble test, helium leak detector).

nodes.name: Silicon
nodes.description: Production of metallurgical-grade silicon, purification to semiconductor grade, Czochralski crystal growth, wafer slicing and polishing, and fabrication of basic solar cells and semiconductor devices. Solar cells are the primary early target — far simpler than logic chips and creating a critical positive feedback loop.
nodes.early_win_note: Solar cells produce power to make more solar cells — the most important positive feedback loop in the entire tech tree

nodes.name: Metallurgical-Grade Silicon Production
nodes.description: Carbothermic reduction of quartz (SiO₂ + 2C → Si + 2CO) in submerged arc electric furnace at 1800-2100°C. Requires 5-30 MW power, produces 97-99% purity MG-Si. Energy consumption: ~11-13 kWh/kg. The starting material for all semiconductor-grade silicon.

nodes.name: Silicon Purification
nodes.description: Chemical purification via Siemens-like process (hydrochlorination → fractional distillation of chlorosilanes → CVD deposition, 9N-11N purity) and physical purification via directional solidification and zone refining (5-9N). Siemens process achieves electronic grade but consumes ~100-200 kWh/kg.

nodes.name: Crystal Growth & Wafering
nodes.description: Czochralski (CZ) crystal pulling from quartz crucibles with precision motion control, wire saw wafering, lapping (2-5 μm TTV), and CMP polishing (<0.5 nm Ra). RCA clean standard for wafer preparation. CZ puller is the most mechanically demanding equipment in the silicon path.

nodes.name: Czochralski Crystal Pulling
nodes.description: Pulling single-crystal silicon ingots from molten polysilicon using a seed crystal in a quartz crucible at ~1415°C. Requires precision leadscrew (±0.5% speed stability), argon atmosphere, and temperature control ±0.5°C. Dash process eliminates dislocations via thin neck growth.

nodes.name: Basic Semiconductor Devices
nodes.description: Solar cell fabrication (p-type wafer → phosphorus diffusion → SiNₓ AR coating → screen-printed metallization, targeting 15-18% efficiency), point-contact diodes, alloy junction transistors, and diffused transistors. Vacuum evaporation and screen printing for metallization.

nodes.name: Photolithography & IC Fabrication
nodes.description: Cleanrooms, photoresist chemistry, mask making, lithography exposure tools, and core fab processes (etch, deposition, doping, metallization). The planar process enables integrated circuits. Expect <1% yield on first IC runs — iteration is essential.

nodes.name: Cleanrooms
nodes.description: Cleanroom construction (ISO 6-4, HEPA/ULPA filtration, laminar flow, positive pressure, gowning protocols), ultra-pure water systems (RO + DI + UV + membrane filtration to 18 MΩ·cm), and cleanroom consumables (garments, wipers, gloves). A single 1 μm particle on a wafer can kill an entire chip.

nodes.name: Photoresists, Masks & Lithography
nodes.description: Photoresist progression from bitumen (100 μm+) to dichromated gelatin (10-50 μm) to novolac+DNQ (0.5-3 μm, positive tone). Spin coating, exposure (mercury lamp g/h/i-line), development in TMAH, and hard bake. Mask making via photoreduction. Contact/proximity and projection printing tools.

nodes.name: Core Fab Processes
nodes.description: Thermal oxidation (dry and wet), wet etching (HF for SiO₂, KOH for Si) and dry/plasma etching (RIE), CVD deposition (poly-Si, SiO₂, Si₃N₄), PVD (sputtering, evaporation), doping (diffusion and later ion implantation), metallization (Al, later Cu), and the planar process integration for IC fabrication. Example NMOS flow with 7 mask layers.

nodes.name: VLSI Scaling & Advanced Semiconductor
nodes.description: Continuous improvement of wafer size (2" → 12"), feature size (10 μm → 7 nm), layer count (1-2 → 10+), and yield (10% → 90%+). Advanced lithography (DUV, eventually EUV), ion implantation, ALD, CMP planarization, copper damascene interconnects, EDA tools, and high-end solar optimization. The pinnacle requiring a mature, self-sustaining industrial ecosystem.
nodes.pinnacle_note: Requires the entire mature ecosystem, generations of accumulated knowledge, and massive capital investment. EUV alone took 20+ years and $10B+ by ASML.

nodes.name: Continuous Scaling
nodes.description: Wafer size progression (2" → 4" → 6" → 8" → 12"), feature size reduction (10 μm → 3 μm → 1 μm → 0.35 μm → 0.13 μm → 7 nm), and metal layer progression (1-2 → 10+). Each generation doubles transistor count and requires better process uniformity, larger equipment, and higher yield.

nodes.name: Advanced Lithography
nodes.description: Projection steppers/scanners (i-line 365 nm → 0.35 μm), DUV excimer lasers (KrF 248 nm, ArF 193 nm with immersion → 0.07 μm), and EUV (13.5 nm from Sn plasma, reflective Mo/Si optics, high vacuum beam path). Chemically amplified resists for DUV. EUV is one of the last achievements — incredibly demanding.

nodes.name: Advanced Processes
nodes.description: Ion implantation (precise doping control), RIE and DRIE etching (Bosch process), atomic layer deposition (ALD, Angstrom-level control), CMP for global planarization, copper damascene interconnects with low-k dielectrics, multi-level metallization, and advanced solar cells (PERC, bifacial, fine-line metallization).

nodes.name: EDA, GPU Design & Advanced Packaging
nodes.description: Electronic Design Automation (logic synthesis, place and route, SPICE simulation, DRC), GPU architecture with billions of transistors, advanced packaging (wire bonding, flip-chip, chiplets, 3D stacking with TSVs), and software stack (drivers, compilers, libraries).

nodes.name: Knowledge Preservation & Education
nodes.description: Writing system development, paper production, movable type printing press, record keeping, technical drawings, education system (apprenticeships → trade schools → universities), library construction, and digital knowledge storage. Without systematic knowledge transmission across 50-200+ years, the entire effort dies with the first generation.
nodes.critical_note: Without knowledge preservation, the 50-200 year bootstrapping effort dies with the first generation. Every process recipe, ore location, and failure mode must be recorded.

nodes.name: Writing & Record-Keeping
nodes.description: Development of writing systems, ink production, and record-keeping materials.

nodes.name: Education & Training
nodes.description: Apprenticeships, trade schools, universities, and systematic knowledge transmission.

nodes.name: Libraries & Information Systems
nodes.description: Library construction, cataloging, preservation, and information retrieval systems.

nodes.name: Transportation & Logistics
nodes.description: Road construction, bridge building, railway systems, water transport, and aviation. Industrial-scale operations require moving materials, people, and information across distances.

nodes.name: Road & Bridge Construction
nodes.description: Road construction from dirt tracks to paved highways, bridge building, and route planning.

nodes.name: Railways
nodes.description: Steam locomotive railways, track construction, signaling, and freight/passenger operations.

nodes.name: Water Transport
nodes.description: Water transport: boats, barges, canals, and maritime shipping infrastructure.

nodes.name: Aviation
nodes.description: Aircraft development, tube-and-fabric airframes, propellers, and flight testing. (Absorbed from former aircraft domain.)

nodes.name: Computing & Automation
nodes.description: Manual and mechanical calculation aids (abacus, slide rules, nomograms, mathematical tables), mechanical calculators (Pascaline, stepped drum, difference engine), automation (cams, linkages, governors, feedback control), automated machine tools (turret lathe, screw machine), and punch card data processing. Bridges the gap until electronic computers arrive.

nodes.name: Mechanical Calculation
nodes.description: Manual and mechanical calculation aids: abacus, slide rules, nomograms, mathematical tables, Pascaline, stepped drum calculators.

nodes.name: Electromechanical Computing
nodes.description: Difference engines, automated machine tools (turret lathe, screw machine), punch card data processing, and tabulating machines.

nodes.name: Electronic Computing
nodes.description: Vacuum tube and early transistor computing, stored-program architecture, and electronic data processing.

nodes.name: Public Health, Sanitation & Medicine
nodes.description: Water supply and purification (wells, slow sand filters, chlorination), sewage treatment, hygiene and quarantine protocols, pharmaceutical production (plant-derived and synthetic), PPE fabrication, surgical capability, and gas detection. Skilled workers are the most valuable and irreplaceable resource.

nodes.name: Water Supply & Sanitation
nodes.description: Water supply, purification, sewage treatment, hygiene protocols, and quarantine systems.

nodes.name: Medicine & Surgery
nodes.description: Surgical capability, wound treatment, infection control, and basic medical diagnostics.

nodes.name: Pharmacology
nodes.description: Plant-derived and synthetic pharmaceutical production, dosage forms, and drug delivery.

nodes.name: Textiles, Fiber & Cordage
nodes.description: Fiber cultivation (flax, hemp, cotton, wool), spinning (drop spindle → treadle wheel), weaving (frame loom → treadle loom), rope-making (hand-laid → rope walk), canvas and sailcloth production, drive belts for power transmission, filter cloth, and protective clothing. Civilization runs on string.
nodes.critical_note: Rope, cordage, and cloth are foundational for all pre-industrial infrastructure — mining hoists, tool hafting, power transmission, and worker protection

nodes.name: Fiber Preparation
nodes.description: Cultivation and preparation of natural fibers including flax, hemp, cotton, and wool.

nodes.name: Spinning
nodes.description: Drop spindle and treadle wheel spinning of fibers into yarn and thread.

nodes.name: Weaving
nodes.description: Frame loom and treadle loom weaving of yarn into cloth, canvas, and sailcloth.

nodes.name: Dyeing
nodes.description: Natural and synthetic dye preparation and application to textiles.

nodes.name: Mining Engineering & Extractive Metallurgy
nodes.description: Surface and underground mining methods, rock breaking (hand tools, fire-setting, black powder blasting), mine ventilation, water pumping (manual → water wheel → Newcomen engine), hoisting and transport, and ore dressing (crushing, gravity separation, magnetic separation). Ore access is the hard constraint on every metallurgical process.
nodes.critical_note: Ore access is the hard constraint on every metallurgical process — the mine drainage → steam engine feedback loop drove all of industrialization

nodes.name: Mining Extraction
nodes.description: Surface and underground mining methods for extracting ore from deposits.

nodes.name: Ore Processing
nodes.description: Ore crushing, grinding, gravity separation, and concentration to produce marketable ore products.

nodes.name: Prospecting & Surveying
nodes.description: Geological survey, ore body identification, and deposit evaluation techniques.

nodes.name: Ceramics & Refractories
nodes.description: Refractory materials (fireclay, high-alumina, silica, magnesia, zirconia, carbon), technical ceramics (alumina, silicon carbide), ceramic processing (ball milling, pressing, sintering, slip casting), ceramic insulation (fiber blanket, insulating firebrick), and semiconductor-specific ceramics (fused silica crucibles, alumina furnace components, ceramic IC packages). Also covers pottery, kilns, and lime production.

nodes.name: Pottery & Clay Products
nodes.description: Clay identification, processing, tempering, forming (pinch, coil, slab), and basic firing. Produces containers, cooking vessels, and refractory linings.

nodes.name: Kiln Firing
nodes.description: Controlled kiln firing reaching 800-900°C for pottery and up to 1400°C for refractory ceramics.

nodes.name: Kiln Construction
nodes.description: Kiln construction: updraft, downdraft, and tunnel kilns for ceramic and lime firing.

nodes.name: Lime Production
nodes.description: Lime production by calcining limestone at 900-1000°C for mortar, whitewash, plaster, and chemical feedstock.

nodes.name: Advanced Ceramics & Refractories
nodes.description: High-performance technical ceramics (alumina, zirconia, silicon carbide, silicon nitride) and refractory linings for furnaces, kilns, and high-temperature industrial processes
nodes.critical_note: Advanced ceramics provide the furnace linings, crucibles, and high-temperature components that make silicon processing and industrial-scale metallurgy possible

nodes.name: Polymers & Composites
nodes.description: Natural rubber vulcanization, synthetic rubbers (nitrile/NBR, neoprene, silicone), thermosetting polymers (Bakelite, epoxy, phenolic/FR-4 laminates, polyester, polyurethane), thermoplastic polymers (polyethylene, PVC, nylon, polystyrene, PTFE), polymer processing equipment (extruders, injection molding), and composite materials (fiberglass, carbon fiber). Provides seals, gaskets, insulation, packaging, and structural materials.

nodes.name: Rubber Production
nodes.description: Natural and synthetic rubber production, vulcanization, and rubber product manufacturing.

nodes.name: Natural Rubber & Vulcanization
nodes.description: Latex collection, coagulation, mastication, compounding with sulfur and carbon black, and vulcanization.

nodes.name: Synthetic Rubbers
nodes.description: Nitrile rubber (NBR), neoprene, and silicone elastomers for oil resistance, wire insulation, and high-temperature seals.

nodes.name: Thermosetting Polymers
nodes.description: Phenol-formaldehyde (Bakelite — electrical insulators, photoresist binders), epoxy resins (structural adhesive, die attach, IC encapsulation), phenolic/epoxy FR-4 laminate (PCB substrate), unsaturated polyester resin (fiberglass matrix), and polyurethane (foams, elastomers).

nodes.name: Thermoplastic Polymers
nodes.description: Polyethylene (LDPE/HDPE — films, containers, wire insulation), PVC (rigid pipe, flexible tubing, requires heat stabilizers), nylon (bearings, gears, engineering components), polystyrene (labware, insulation foam), and PTFE/Teflon (chemically inert, ultra-low friction, -200°C to +260°C, for seals and non-stick surfaces).

nodes.name: Composite Materials
nodes.description: Fiberglass production (E-glass fiber from silica/boron batch, 5-25 μm filaments), hand layup, vacuum bagging, resin transfer molding, and filament winding. Carbon fiber (PAN precursor, aspiration). Polymer processing equipment: extruders, injection molding machines, compression presses, and calenders.

nodes.name: Glass
nodes.description: Glass production from basic soda-lime through advanced specialty glasses. Spans natural glass and simple melt forming, borosilicate for thermal shock resistance, fused silica for optical purity, and quartz crucibles for semiconductor crystal growth. Glass is both a structural material (vessels, tubing) and a functional enabler (optical elements, semiconductor consumables).

nodes.name: Optics
nodes.description: Optical component manufacturing and inspection systems. Spans lens grinding and polishing, compound microscopes for defect inspection, optical comparators for dimensional measurement, and spectroscopes for material analysis. Optics enables both quality inspection of manufactured parts and the projection imaging essential for photolithography alignment.

nodes.name: Gas Handling
nodes.description: Gas compression, storage, purification, and vacuum systems. Spans basic gas handling with piping and compression, through vacuum pumps and chambers, to leak detection and controlled atmosphere environments. Essential for semiconductor fabrication processes requiring inert atmospheres, vacuum deposition, and precise gas delivery.

nodes.name: Measurement & Instrumentation
nodes.description: Precision measurement, instrumentation, and calibration systems. Spans temperature and pressure sensing, dimensional metrology via the Whitworth three-plate method, gauge blocks and micrometers, electrical standards and calibration chains. You cannot build what you cannot measure — this domain provides the quantitative foundation for all precision manufacturing.

nodes.name: Optical Inspection
nodes.description: Lens grinding and polishing (rough grind → fine grind → polish with cerium oxide), compound microscopes (40x-1000x), optical comparators, and spectroscopes. Essential for crystal defect inspection and lithography alignment.

nodes.name: Temperature & Pressure Measurement
nodes.description: Thermocouples, RTDs, pressure gauges, and electrical measurement instruments (galvanometers, Wheatstone bridges). Provides the thermal and pressure sensing needed for furnace control, crystal growth, and process monitoring.

nodes.name: Distillation
nodes.description: Distillation columns (2-10 m, copper or steel, packed with ceramic Raschig rings), batch and continuous flow reactors, and shell-and-tube heat exchangers. Separates liquid mixtures by boiling point for chemical purification and petroleum refining.

nodes.name: Basic Gas Handling
nodes.description: Piping, valves, and pumps for corrosive service, gas compression and storage, and purification by scrubbing, molecular sieves, and catalytic getters. Provides the gas distribution infrastructure needed across chemical processing and semiconductor fabrication.

nodes.name: Steam Turbines
nodes.description: Continuous rotary power from expanding steam via impulse (De Laval) and reaction (Parsons) turbine stages. 50-500+ MW output at 30-40% thermal efficiency with superheat and condenser. Replaces reciprocating engines for all large-scale power generation beyond ~5 MW.

nodes.name: Water Turbines
nodes.description: Rotary hydraulic turbines converting water head to shaft power: Pelton (impulse, 30-1000+ m head, 85-91% efficient), Francis (reaction, 3-300 m head, 80-94%), Kaplan (axial-flow, 1-15 m head, 80-92%). Require iron/steel and precision machining — qualitatively different technology from water wheels.

nodes.name: Pumped Hydroelectric Storage
nodes.description: Reversible pump-turbine systems storing energy by pumping water uphill to a reservoir, recovering 70-85% via turbine generation on release. The most mature grid-scale energy storage technology, essential for matching intermittent supply with demand.

nodes.name: Graphite Electrode Manufacturing
nodes.description: Manufacturing of graphite electrodes from petroleum coke and coal tar pitch via extrusion, baking (800-1200°C), and graphitization (2500-3000°C). Bootstrapping challenge: need electrodes for EAF but need EAF for graphitization. Amorphous carbon electrodes bridge the gap.

nodes.name: Blast Furnace and Cast Iron
nodes.description: Continuous iron smelting in tall shaft furnaces producing liquid pig iron (~4% C, ~1% Si) at 1200-1500°C. Cupola furnace remelts pig iron for casting. Three cast iron types: gray (excellent castability, machinable), white (hard, wear-resistant), malleable (heat-treated white iron, tougher). Produces 10-1000+ tonnes per day versus 5-20 kg per bloomery cycle.

nodes.name: Black Powder Explosives
nodes.description: Gunpowder manufacture (75% KNO₃, 15% charcoal, 10% sulfur) for mining blasting. Process: pulverize ingredients separately, wet-mix, press into cakes, crumble and sieve to grain sizes. Corning process ensures uniform burn rate. Enables breaking rock far beyond fire-setting and hand-tool limits.

nodes.name: Telegraph Communication
nodes.description: Electrical telegraph systems using Morse code over iron or copper wire for long-distance communication. Hub-and-spoke networks with relay stations every 50-100 km. Components: telegraph key, sounder, Daniell cell batteries, pole-line construction. Transforms coordination from days-by-courier to minutes-by-wire. Critical for railway block signaling.

nodes.name: Paper and Printing Press
nodes.description: Paper production from wood pulp or cotton/linen rag, and movable-type printing press for mass reproduction of text. Gutenberg-style press produces 200-500 impressions per hour. Essential for distributing technical knowledge: process recipes, engineering drawings, failure logs, educational materials across settlements and generations.
nodes.early_win_note: Printing press multiplies knowledge distribution speed by 100-500x, enabling rapid dissemination of process recipes, engineering drawings, and failure logs across all settlements

nodes.name: Explosives and Propellants
nodes.description: Manufacture of explosive and propellant compounds: black powder (KNO₃/charcoal/sulfur), nitrocellulose/guncotton (cellulose + nitric acid), dynamite (nitroglycerin absorbed in diatomaceous earth), and smokeless powders. Enables efficient mining beyond hand tools, civil engineering (tunnels, canals), and military applications.

nodes.name: Fermentation Chemistry
nodes.description: Microbial and chemical production of solvents and fuels without petroleum: ethanol via grain malting/mashing/fermentation/distillation, acetone-butanol via Weizmann process (Clostridium acetobutylicum), acetic acid via vinegar oxidation or catalytic route, methanol from wood pyrolysis or synthesis gas. Critical non-petroleum chemical feedstock path.

nodes.name: Wood Gasification
nodes.description: Producer gas generation from biomass via shaft gasifier: partial combustion produces CO+H₂+N₂ mixture (~5-6 MJ/m³). Gas cleaning via water scrubbing, sawdust filter, cyclone. Can power internal combustion engines directly. Powered vehicles during WWII fuel shortages. Bridge technology before natural gas infrastructure.

nodes.name: Glassblowing and Scientific Apparatus
nodes.description: Lampworking and freehand glassblowing for laboratory and technical apparatus. Torch types (Bunsen, gas-oxygen, hydrogen-oxygen). Standard products: round-bottom flasks, condensers, distillation columns, thermometers, vacuum manifolds, ampoules. Glass-to-metal seals for electrical feedthroughs. Essential for ALL chemistry lab work beyond test tubes.

nodes.name: Gutta-Percha Processing
nodes.description: Trans-1,4-polyisoprene from Palaquium tree latex. Hard, horn-like thermoplastic at room temperature, softens at 60-80°C. Harvested by felling trees and collecting coagulated latex. Processing: wash, masticate, roll into sheets. The ONLY practical underwater electrical insulation until polyethylene (1940s). Used for submarine telegraph cables, dental fillings, tool handles, electrical insulation.

nodes.name: Shellac Production
nodes.description: Lac bug (Kerria lacca) secretion resin, dissolved in ethanol. Harvested as sticklac from host trees, washed to remove dye (yielding seedlac), melted and filtered into buttonlac or flake shellac. French polish for wood finishing. Uses: phonograph records (before vinyl), electrical insulation, primer/sealer, food coating, gasket material. Fastest-drying natural finish.

nodes.name: Rope and Cordage
nodes.description: Manufacture of rope from natural and synthetic fibers via ropewalk or mechanized spinner. 3-strand twist rope construction: fibers → yarn → strand → rope. Splicing techniques (eye splice, back splice, short splice) stronger than knots. Tarring for weather resistance. Breaking strength tables for hemp, manila, nylon. Essential for construction hoisting, sailing, mining, and all mechanical handling.

nodes.name: Optical Coatings
nodes.description: Thin-film deposition for optical enhancement: MgF₂ anti-reflection coating (quarter-wave, ~100 nm, thermal evaporation at 10⁻⁴ Pa) and aluminum mirror coating (vacuum evaporation, ~100 nm, 88-92% reflectance). Requires vacuum deposition chamber, tungsten/tantalum boats, thickness monitoring. Enables multi-element lens systems and protected mirrors.

nodes.name: Plants & Botanical Resources
nodes.description: Cultivation, foraging, and utilization of plant species for food, medicine, fiber, structural materials, and dyes. Plants provide the primary biomass inputs for survival and early industry — calories, healing compounds, cordage fibers, timber, and colorants.
nodes.early_win_note: Wild edible and medicinal plants are available from Year 0, providing immediate food security and basic healthcare without cultivation

nodes.name: Edible Plants
nodes.description: Foraging and cultivation of plant species for human consumption including vegetables, fruits, seeds, roots, and herbs. Provides caloric intake and essential nutrients from wild and domesticated sources.
nodes.early_win_note: Wild edible plants provide immediate food security before agriculture is established

nodes.name: Medicinal Plants
nodes.description: Identification, preparation, and application of plant species with therapeutic properties for treating illness, injury, and disease. Foundation of all pharmacology.
nodes.early_win_note: Basic herbal remedies available from Year 0 reduce mortality and improve labor productivity

nodes.name: Fiber Plants
nodes.description: Cultivation and harvesting of plant species that yield fibers for cordage, textiles, and weaving. Source materials for rope, thread, and fabric production.

nodes.name: Structural Plants
nodes.description: Harvesting of plant species used for construction, building materials, and structural applications including timber, thatch, and woven structures.

nodes.name: Dye Plants
nodes.description: Cultivation and processing of plant species that yield natural colorants for dyeing textiles, leather, and other materials. Source of indigo, madder, weld, and other traditional dyes.

nodes.name: Allium ampeloprasum
nodes.description: Allium ampeloprasum is a member of the onion genus Allium . The wild plant is commonly known as wild leek or broadleaf wild leek . Its native range includes southern Europe, southwestern Asia and North Africa, but it has been cultivated and naturalized in many other countries

nodes.name: Artemisia absinthium
nodes.description: Artemisia absinthium , otherwise known as common wormwood , is a species of Artemisia native to North Africa and temperate regions of Eurasia , and widely naturalized in Canada and the northern United States

nodes.name: Agave
nodes.description: Agave

nodes.name: Bitter orange
nodes.description: The bitter orange , sour orange , Seville orange , bigarade orange , or marmalade orange is the hybrid citrus tree species Citrus × aurantium , and its fruit. It is native to Southeast Asia and has been spread by humans to many parts of the world

nodes.name: Balanites aegyptiaca
nodes.description: Balanites aegyptiaca (also known as the Desert date , Egyptian balsam and Lalob in Sudan ) is a species of tree, classified as a member of either the Zygophyllaceae or the Balanitaceae

nodes.name: Agrimonia pilosa
nodes.description: Agrimonia pilosa also known as hairy agrimony , is a flowering plant in the family Rosaceae . It is distributed primarily over the Korean Peninsula , Japan, China, Siberia , and Eastern Europe

nodes.name: Aloe officinalis
nodes.description: Aloe officinalis is a species of aloe in the Asphodelaceae family. The species is native to the Arabian Peninsula , from Yemen to Saudi Arabia . The plant has become naturalized in Somalia and has medicinal value. The plant also has two varieties:

nodes.name: Artemisia douglasiana
nodes.description: Artemisia douglasiana , known as California mugwort , Douglas's sagewort , or dream plant , is a western North American species of aromatic herb in the sunflower family

nodes.name: Artemisia inculta
nodes.description: Artemisia inculta is a rare perennial species of aromatic undershrub in the family Asteraceae , native to North Africa and select regions of the Middle East

nodes.name: Botryodiscia
nodes.description: Botryodiscia is a genus of flowering plants in the family Menispermaceae . It includes a single species, Botryodiscia tetrandra , is a herbaceous perennial vine or scrambling subshrub native native to southern China, Hainan, Taiwan, and Vietnam

nodes.name: Allium paniculatum
nodes.description: Allium paniculatum , common name pale garlic , is a species of monocot in the family Amaryllidaceae . It is widely cultivated and is now naturalized in several places outside its native range. Allium paniculatum produces several egg-shaped bulbs, each up to 1

nodes.name: Amaranthus albus
nodes.description: Amaranthus albus is an annual species of flowering plant native to the Americas. Its common names include common tumbleweed , tumble pigweed , tumbleweed , prostrate pigweed , pigweed amaranth , white amaranth and white pigweed

nodes.name: Artemisia capillaris
nodes.description: Artemisia capillaris , (Chinese: 茵陈蒿 yīn chén hāo), the capillary wormwood , is a species of flowering plant in the wormwood genus Artemisia , family Asteraceae

nodes.name: Ammonia & Fertilizer Production
nodes.description: Haber-Bosch synthesis of ammonia (N2 + 3H2 → 2NH3) at 400-500°C, 15-30 MPa over Fe catalyst. Ostwald process oxidation to nitric acid. Fertilizer production: ammonium nitrate, urea, ammonium sulfate, superphosphate. Feeds half the world's population.

nodes.name: Soap & Detergent Production
nodes.description: Saponification of fats/oils with NaOH or KOH to produce soap and glycerol. Industrial continuous saponification, spray drying, synthetic detergents (LAS). Glycerol recovery for explosives and pharmaceuticals.

nodes.name: Solvent Production
nodes.description: Industrial solvents: ethanol, methanol, diethyl ether, turpentine, benzene/toluene/xylene, hydrogen peroxide (anthraquinone process), chlorinated solvents, carbon disulfide. Solvent selection by polarity, boiling point, toxicity. Recovery via distillation.

nodes.name: Refractory Materials
nodes.description: Firebrick, alumina, magnesite, and insulating refractories for furnace linings above 1500°C. Crucible production (clay-graphite, SiC). Castable refractories and kiln furniture. Essential for metals, glass, cement, and ceramic kilns.

nodes.name: Paints, Coatings & Inks
nodes.description: Paint chemistry (pigment + binder + solvent), nitrocellulose dope for aircraft fabric, varnishes and lacquers, ink chemistry (iron gall, carbon, printing inks), rust prevention primers, heat-resistant coatings.

nodes.name: Pulp & Paper Chemicals
nodes.description: Kraft pulping chemistry (white liquor NaOH+Na2S), bleaching (ClO2, H2O2), sizing agents (alum, AKD, rosin), fillers (CaCO3, kaolin, TiO2). Recovery boiler cycle: black liquor → green liquor → white liquor.

nodes.name: Modern Steelmaking
nodes.description: Conversion of pig iron and scrap into steel via Bessemer, open-hearth, basic oxygen furnace (BOF), and electric arc furnace (EAF). Ladle metallurgy for secondary refining. Continuous casting for near-net-shape solidification. The dominant process chain for producing 1.9 billion tonnes of steel annually.
nodes.critical_note: Modern steelmaking produces the structural material for machinery, construction, transport, and all industrial infrastructure

nodes.name: Aluminum Production
nodes.description: Bayer process converts bauxite to alumina; Hall-Héroult electrolytic reduction of alumina in molten cryolite at 960-980°C produces aluminum at 13-15 kWh/kg. Aluminum alloys (1xxx-8xxx series) for structural, aerospace, electrical, and packaging applications. Extrusion, die casting, and 95% energy savings from recycling.

nodes.name: Non-Ferrous Metal Production
nodes.description: Industrial production of zinc, lead, tin, nickel, magnesium, and titanium from their ores. Processes include roasting, reduction, electrolysis, distillation (zinc), and the Kroll process (titanium). Enables galvanizing (zinc), batteries (lead), solder (tin), stainless steel and superalloys (nickel), lightweight structural parts (magnesium), and aerospace/medical implants (titanium).

nodes.name: Specialty Alloys
nodes.description: Alloy steels (stainless, tool steels, Hadfield manganese), superalloys (Ni-based Inconel, Co-based Stellite), titanium alloys (Ti-6Al-4V), and specialty copper alloys (beryllium copper, phosphor bronze). Alloy design principles: solid solution strengthening, precipitation hardening, Hall-Petch grain size relationship. Enables gas turbine blades, cutting tools, chemical plant equipment, and aerospace structures.

nodes.name: Primary Metal Forming
nodes.description: Bulk primary forming at the smelter and mill: continuous casting (strand solidification from molten steel), hot rolling (slab/billet to sheet/plate/structural), extrusion (aluminum profiles), wire rod rolling, forging at scale (open-die hydraulic press, closed-die), and structural section rolling (I-beams, rails). Transforms raw metal into stock shapes for downstream manufacturing.

nodes.name: Metal Finishing & Surface Treatment
nodes.description: Surface treatment processes for corrosion protection and functional properties: galvanizing (hot-dip zinc), electroplating (Cr, Ni, Cu, Sn, Au), electroless plating (Ni-P), anodizing (aluminum oxide), phosphating (steel conversion coating), case hardening (carburizing, nitriding), passivation (stainless steel), thermal spraying (HVOF, plasma), and pickling/descaling.

nodes.name: Powder Metallurgy & Advanced Materials
nodes.description: Production of metal powders (atomization, reduction, carbonyl), consolidation (pressing, sintering), and advanced processes including cemented carbides (WC-Co cutting tools), tungsten and refractory metals, metal injection molding (MIM), hot isostatic pressing (HIP), and additive manufacturing (laser powder bed fusion, electron beam melting). Enables impossible-to-cast alloys, near-net-shape parts, and highest-performance cutting tools.

nodes.name: Agriculture
nodes.description: Cultivation of food crops, management of soil nutrients, and controlled-environment growing systems. Provides the caloric foundation for civilization — without reliable food production, no specialist labor force exists to develop advanced technology.
nodes.critical_note: Food production is the prerequisite for all surplus-dependent civilization development
nodes.early_win_note: Reliable agriculture creates food surplus → specialist labor → faster technological progress

nodes.name: Animals & Animal Husbandry
nodes.description: Domestication, husbandry, and utilization of animals for food, fiber, motive power, transport, and materials. Animals provide critical inputs across the tech tree: draft power (the primary motive force before steam), leather (durable flexible material), wool (insulating fiber), tallow (first lubricant and candle fuel), bone and horn (tool materials), sinew (high-tensile cordage), and dairy products.
nodes.early_win_note: Hunting and trapping provide immediate food and materials; domestication creates the surplus engine that drives civilization

nodes.name: Livestock Domestication & Husbandry
nodes.description: Selective capture, taming, breeding, and management of cattle, sheep, goats, pigs, poultry, horses, and donkeys. Each species provides distinct products: cattle (draft power, milk, leather, tallow), sheep (wool, milk, meat), goats (milk, hides, brush clearing), pigs (meat, tallow, waste conversion), poultry (eggs, pest control, meat), horses and donkeys (fast transport, heavy draft).
nodes.critical_note: Without animal domestication, plowing and transport are limited to human muscle until steam engines arrive; manure is essential for sustained agriculture

nodes.name: Animal Draft Power & Harnessing
nodes.description: Yoke and harness systems for oxen, horses, and mules. Applications: plowing (ox-drawn plow breaks sod that hand tools cannot), cart and wagon pulling (500-1500 kg per animal), water lifting (animal-powered sakia), grain milling (horse or ox-powered millstones), mining hoisting (horse whim: 3-4 HP). Primary motive power source until steam engines.
nodes.early_win_note: Draft animals multiply human labor 5-10x for plowing and transport, creating surplus with minimal technology

nodes.name: Animal-Derived Materials Processing
nodes.description: Processing animal products into useful materials: leather (dehairing with lime, tanning with oak bark tannins, oil tanning, currying), tallow and lard rendering (fat melted at 80-100°C, clarified), wool preparation (shearing, scouring, carding), horn and bone working (heat-softening, carving, boiling for glue), sinew processing (drying, pounding into fibers), hide glue production, and blood processing.

nodes.name: Apiculture (Beekeeping)
nodes.description: Log hive and skep beekeeping for honey and beeswax production. Honey serves as food preservative, antibacterial wound treatment, fermentation substrate for mead, and pill binder. Beeswax is critical for investment casting (enabling precision bronze parts), writing tablets, candle fuel, waterproofing, belt dressing, and dental fillings. Pollination services increase crop yields 20-30%.
nodes.early_win_note: Pollination alone increases crop yields 20-30%, and beeswax is critical for investment casting enabling precision bronze parts

nodes.name: Aquaculture & Fisheries
nodes.description: Fish farming and capture fisheries including pond construction, fish stocking, net making, trap construction, and fish processing (drying, smoking, salting). Provides high-quality protein from aquatic systems that do not compete with land-based agriculture.

nodes.name: Sericulture (Silkworm Rearing)
nodes.description: Rearing silkworms (Bombyx mori) on mulberry leaves, cocoon harvesting, and silk thread production. Silk is the strongest natural fiber by weight, used for high-value textiles, medical sutures, and parachutes. Requires established domestication skills and controlled feeding.

nodes.name: Biological Pest Management
nodes.description: Biological pest control using domesticated animals: cats for rodent control in granaries, geese for weed control in crops, ducks for insect pest control in rice paddies, and guardian dogs for livestock protection from predators. Integrated approach combining animal behavior with crop protection.

nodes.name: Water Procurement
nodes.description: Finding, collecting, storing, and transporting water for drinking, sanitation, and agriculture. Water finding by vegetation and geological indicators, rainwater harvesting, spring development, and basic transport infrastructure predating pumped distribution systems.

nodes.name: Fabric Finishing
nodes.description: Post-weaving fabric treatments including fulling, napping, calendering, singeing, mercerization, waterproofing, and fireproofing. Transforms raw cloth into functional textiles for specific applications.

nodes.name: Sewing & Tailoring
nodes.description: Garment construction, needle making, thread selection, seam types, and industrial sewing techniques. Converts raw cloth into clothing, bags, belts, tents, sails, and technical textile products.

nodes.name: Mechanical Drilling
nodes.description: Mechanical drilling techniques for production blast holes and exploration core sampling. Jackhammer drilling, diamond core drilling, and rotary drilling for both mining operations and geological survey.

nodes.name: Mine Ventilation
nodes.description: Underground ventilation engineering for air supply, gas dilution, heat management, and dust control. Furnace ventilation, fan ventilation, and ventilation network design using Atkinson's equation.

nodes.name: Glass Fiber Production
nodes.description: Glass fiber production for insulation wool and textile-grade fiberglass reinforcement. Rotary fiberization for insulation, bushing drawing for continuous filaments, and sizing application for composite reinforcement.

nodes.name: Electrical Measurement Instruments
nodes.description: Voltmeters, ammeters, ohmmeters, Wheatstone bridges, galvanometers, oscilloscopes, and signal generators. Essential for energy, chemistry, and semiconductor domains.

nodes.name: Optical Measurement Instruments
nodes.description: Microscopes, telescopes, spectrometers, autocollimators, and interferometers used as precision measurement tools. Bridges optics domain to measurement for semiconductor inspection and scientific instrumentation.

nodes.name: Precision Optical Instruments
nodes.description: Interferometers, autocollimators, optical flats, and spherometers for precision optical metrology. Used for lens testing, flatness verification, and angular measurement in semiconductor and scientific applications.

nodes.name: Occupational Health & Industrial Hygiene
nodes.description: Industrial hygiene, workplace exposure limits, noise control, ergonomics, respiratory protection, and occupational disease prevention. Essential for workforce survival in industrial environments.

nodes.name: Medical Instrument Manufacturing
nodes.description: Surgical instrument manufacturing, autoclave design and operation, suture production, and basic medical equipment fabrication. Bridges metals and health domains for healthcare tooling.

nodes.name: Digital Logic Design
nodes.description: Boolean algebra, combinational logic gates, sequential logic, state machines, and register transfer level design. Foundation for all digital computing from relay logic to integrated circuits.

nodes.name: Data Storage Systems
nodes.description: Evolution of data storage from punch cards and paper tape through magnetic tape, magnetic drum, disk storage, and core memory. Each technology provides increasing density and access speed.

nodes.name: Wafer Slicing & Polishing
nodes.description: Wafer production from crystal ingots: wire saw slicing, lapping, chemical mechanical polishing (CMP), and RCA cleaning. Transforms grown crystals into device-ready wafers with mirror-smooth surfaces.

nodes.name: Semiconductor-Grade Electrolysis
nodes.description: Membrane electrolysis cells producing ultra-pure chlorine and caustic soda for semiconductor manufacturing. Uses ion-exchange membranes instead of asbestos diaphragms, achieving higher purity required for wafer processing chemicals.

nodes.name: Water Electrolysis
nodes.description: Electrolytic splitting of water into hydrogen and oxygen using PEM or alkaline electrolyzers. Provides high-purity hydrogen for ammonia synthesis, metal refining, and fuel cells without fossil fuel inputs.

nodes.name: Redox Flow Battery
nodes.description: Vanadium or zinc-bromine redox flow batteries for grid-scale energy storage. Stores energy in liquid electrolytes separated by ion-exchange membranes, enabling decoupled power and energy scaling for industrial load management.

nodes.name: Fuel Cell
nodes.description: Proton exchange membrane fuel cells converting hydrogen and oxygen to electricity, water, and heat. Provides efficient distributed power generation for applications requiring clean, quiet energy conversion.

nodes.name: E-Methanol Synthesis
nodes.description: Catalytic synthesis of methanol from captured CO₂ and green hydrogen using membrane reactors. Provides a liquid energy carrier and chemical feedstock that is transportable and storable without high-pressure infrastructure.

nodes.name: Electrodialysis
nodes.description: Ion separation using alternating cation and anion exchange membranes under electric field. Desalinates brackish water, recovers acids and bases from salt solutions, and concentrates process streams for industrial chemistry.

nodes.name: Desalination
nodes.description: Reverse osmosis and electrodialysis desalination of seawater and brackish water using semipermeable membranes. Produces fresh water for agriculture, industry, and human consumption in water-scarce regions.

nodes.name: Blue Energy (Osmotic Power)
nodes.description: Pressure-retarded osmosis and reverse electrodialysis generating electricity from the salinity gradient between freshwater and seawater. Uses ion-selective membranes to convert chemical potential difference into electrical power.

nodes.name: Hydroponic pH Control
nodes.description: Ion-selective membrane systems for precise nutrient and pH management in hydroponic agriculture. Maintains optimal nutrient solution composition through controlled ion exchange, enabling soil-free high-yield food production.

nodes.name: Lithium Separation
nodes.description: Selective lithium extraction from brines and clays using lithium-ion selective membranes and electrodialysis. Produces battery-grade lithium compounds essential for energy storage and electronics manufacturing.

nodes.name: Acid Regeneration
nodes.description: Membrane-based recovery and regeneration of spent acids from industrial processes. Uses diffusion dialysis and electrodialysis to separate acid from dissolved metal salts, enabling closed-loop acid usage in metal processing and chemical manufacturing.

nodes.name: Water
nodes.description: Water supply, purification, desalination, and hydraulic systems. Clean water is fundamental to agriculture, sanitation, and industrial processes — every stage of civilization bootstrapping depends on reliable water access.

nodes.name: Poultry Farming
nodes.description: Managed rearing of domesticated birds for eggs, meat, feathers, and pest control services. Poultry species convert feed and forage into protein more efficiently than most livestock, and many species provide complementary services such as pest control, weed management, and guardian behavior.
nodes.early_win_note: Chickens and quail reproduce rapidly, providing eggs and meat within months of establishment. Poultry manure is the highest-nitrogen livestock manure, immediately improving crop yields.

nodes.name: Chickens
nodes.description: Domesticated fowl (Gallus gallus domesticus) raised for eggs and meat. The most widely kept poultry species, producing 200-300 eggs per year for modern layers on just 100-150g feed/day. Dual-purpose breeds provide both eggs and meat, reaching slaughter weight at 12-16 weeks on range.
nodes.early_win_note: Point-of-lay at 18-22 weeks, eggs within first year. Minimal space requirements and efficient feed conversion make chickens the fastest livestock to establish.

nodes.name: Coturnix Quail
nodes.description: Japanese quail (Coturnix japonica) raised for eggs and meat. Sexual maturity at 6 weeks, producing 300+ eggs per year at 10-12g each. The smallest and fastest-reproducing poultry species, requiring minimal space (25x25 cm cage per bird) and feed (20-25g/day).
nodes.early_win_note: Egg production begins at 6 weeks — the fastest return of any poultry. Virtually silent, suitable for urban or stealth setups.

nodes.name: Ducks
nodes.description: Domesticated ducks (Anas platyrhynchos domesticus, Cairina moschata) raised for eggs, meat, and pest control. Khaki Campbell ducks produce 250-340 eggs per year, exceeding most chicken breeds. Ducks are superior foragers of slugs, snails, and aquatic plants, and are more disease-resistant than chickens.
nodes.early_win_note: Excellent foragers that control slug and snail populations while producing more eggs than most chicken breeds.

nodes.name: Geese
nodes.description: Domesticated geese (Anser anser domesticus) raised for meat, down, eggs, and weed control. Geese are primarily grazers that can subsist on pasture with minimal supplemental feed, making them the most feed-efficient poultry for meat production on grassland. Their aggressive alarm behavior provides guardian functions.

nodes.name: Turkeys
nodes.description: Domesticated turkeys (Meleagris gallopavo) raised for meat. Toms reach 8-15 kg, hens 4-8 kg, with market weight at 14-20 weeks. Turkeys require more space than chickens (20+ m² per bird for range) and are susceptible to blackhead disease, requiring separation from chickens.

nodes.name: Guinea Fowl
nodes.description: Helmeted guinea fowl (Numida meleagris) raised for meat, eggs, and pest control. Guinea fowl are specialist consumers of ticks, insects, and small rodents, providing exceptional pest control services. Their loud alarm calls serve as a guardian function for other poultry. Extremely hardy and disease-resistant.
nodes.early_win_note: Outstanding tick and insect control — a flock of guinea fowl can dramatically reduce tick-borne disease risk in the settlement area.

nodes.name: Pigeons & Squab
nodes.description: Domestic pigeons (Columba livia) raised for squab meat (26-30 day old juveniles) and guano fertilizer. Pigeons are self-feeding foragers requiring minimal supplemental feed, and squab reaches market weight (350-500g dressed) in under a month. Pigeon guano is one of the richest natural fertilizers (high NPK).
nodes.early_win_note: Near-zero feed cost if allowed to forage. Squab production cycle is under 30 days. Guano is premium fertilizer.

nodes.name: Cattle Husbandry
nodes.description: Raising domesticated cattle (Bos taurus) for dairy, beef, tallow, leather, and draft power. Cattle are the largest and most productive livestock species, yielding 10-20 L milk/day at peak lactation and 275-300 kg dressed carcass. Oxen provide primary draft power for plowing and transport before mechanization.
nodes.critical_note: Cattle provide the primary draft power for pre-industrial agriculture and transport, plus dairy as the most efficient large-scale protein source

nodes.name: Sheep Husbandry
nodes.description: Raising domesticated sheep (Ovis aries) for wool, mutton, and dairy. Sheep produce 2-5 kg fleece per shearing (60-70% clean fiber yield), reach slaughter weight at 4-6 months, and provide nitrogen-rich manure (0.7% N fresh weight). Wool is the primary fiber for textiles before cotton and synthetics.
nodes.early_win_note: Sheep produce wool within the first year, enabling textile production. Fast reproduction (twins common) builds flock quickly.

nodes.name: Goat Husbandry
nodes.description: Raising domesticated goats (Capra aegagrus hircus) for milk, meat, fiber, and brush clearance. Goats are browsers that clear woody undergrowth other livestock avoid, produce milk with higher digestibility than cow milk, and thrive on marginal forage. Dual-purpose breeds provide both milk and meat efficiently.
nodes.early_win_note: Goats clear brush and browse on marginal land unsuitable for cattle or sheep, converting scrub into milk and meat. Twin kids are the norm.

nodes.name: Pig Husbandry
nodes.description: Raising domesticated pigs (Sus scrofa domesticus) for pork, lard, and waste disposal. Pigs convert feed to carcass more efficiently than cattle or sheep (70-80% dressed yield), consume food waste and crop residues other livestock reject, and produce 8-12 piglets per litter. The most productive omnivore in the livestock stable.
nodes.early_win_note: Market weight (90-110 kg) at 5-7 months. Pigs convert kitchen waste and crop residues into meat and lard, closing the food waste loop.

nodes.name: Equine Husbandry
nodes.description: Raising horses (Equus caballus) and donkeys (Equus africanus asinus) for draft power, transport, and pack carrying. Horses provide faster transport and greater pulling power; donkeys are more enduring, tolerate poor forage, and have longer working lifespans (25-30 years vs 15-20 for horses). Both species are essential for pre-industrial motive power.
nodes.critical_note: Equines and cattle together provide all pre-industrial motive power — without draft animals, agriculture and transport are limited to human labor alone

nodes.name: Camelid Husbandry
nodes.description: Raising alpacas (Vicugna pacos) and llamas (Lama glama) for fiber, pack transport, and guard duty. Alpacas produce 2-5 kg of fine fiber every 12-18 months; llamas carry 25-30% of body weight as pack animals and serve as livestock guardians against predators. Both are hardy, disease-resistant, and use communal dung piles for easy manure collection.

nodes.name: Bison Husbandry
nodes.description: Raising American bison (Bison bison) for meat and hides on grassland. Bison are naturally hardy with minimal health issues, requiring no shelter in most climates and finishing on grass alone. Their meat is leaner and higher in protein than beef. Handling requires reinforced facilities due to wild temperament and 1.8m jumping ability.

nodes.name: Rabbit Husbandry
nodes.description: Raising domesticated rabbits (Oryctolagus cuniculus) for meat, fur, and Angora wool. Rabbits produce 4-6 litters of 6-12 kits per year, reaching processing weight (2-2.5 kg) at 10-12 weeks on minimal space (0.15-0.25 m² per kg). Rabbit manure can be applied directly to crops without composting (cold manure).
nodes.early_win_note: Fastest reproduction of any livestock — a single doe can produce 100+ kg of meat per year. Minimal space, feed, and infrastructure requirements.

nodes.name: Insect Farming (BSF)
nodes.description: Farming Black Soldier Fly larvae (Hermetia illucens) for protein meal and biowaste conversion. BSF larvae convert 1 ton of organic waste into 150-200 kg of fresh larvae (40-45% protein dry weight) in 14-18 days, providing high-protein feed for chickens, fish, and pigs while reducing waste volume by 50-80%.
nodes.early_win_note: Converts food waste and manure into high-protein animal feed within 2 weeks. The fastest protein production cycle in any livestock system.

nodes.name: Soil Management & Composting
nodes.description: Systematic management of soil fertility through composting, cover cropping, and organic matter amendment. Composting converts animal manure, crop residues, and food waste into stable humus at 55-65°C, killing pathogens and weed seeds. Sustained crop yields depend on replacing nutrients removed by harvest.
nodes.critical_note: Without soil fertility management, crop yields decline 2-5% per year as nutrients are depleted, eventually making agriculture unsustainable
nodes.early_win_note: Composting animal manure and crop waste produces safe, stable fertilizer within 3-6 months, immediately improving crop yields 15-25%.

nodes.name: Vermiculture
nodes.description: Composting with Eisenia fetida (red wiggler) earthworms to produce vermicompost and worm castings. Worm castings contain 1.5-2.5% N, 1.0-1.5% P, and 1.0-1.5% K — significantly richer than thermophilic compost. Worm populations double every 2-3 months under ideal conditions, and worm tea serves as a potent liquid fertilizer.
nodes.early_win_note: Small-scale vermicomposting can be established in weeks with a bucket of worms and household food scraps, producing premium fertilizer continuously.

nodes.name: Aquaponics
nodes.description: Integrated fish and plant production system where fish waste provides nutrients for plants, and plants filter water for fish. Aquaponics uses natural biological nitrogen cycling (ammonia → nitrite → nitrate) without chemical pH adjustment, producing both fish protein and vegetables in a single recirculating system. Simpler and lower-tech than hydroponic pH control systems.
